title: Smt Thimmakka vs Sri S K Lakshmana on 2 November, 2012
body: Smt Thimmakka vs Sri S K Lakshmana on 2 November, 2012 Author: B.Sreenivase Gowda Bench: B. Sreenivase Gowda 1 IN THE HIGH COURT OF KARNATAKA AT BANGALORE DATED THIS THE 2ND DAY OF NOVEMBER 2012 BEFORE THE HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE B. SREENIVASE GOWDA R.S.A. NO.1638 OF 2008 C/W. R.S.A. NO.1616 OF 2008 R.S.A. NO.1638 OF 2008 BETWEEN : 1. SMT. THIMMAKKA, MAJOR, W/O LATE SANNAPPA, AGED ABOUT 67 YEARS, 2. SRI. MANJUNATHA, AGED ABOUT 37 YEARS, S/O. LATE SANNAPPA, 3. SRI. SURESH @ SURI, S/O LATE SANNAPPA, AGED ABOUT 32 YEARS, 4. SRI. RAJA, AGED ABOUT 30 YEARS, S/O. LATE SANNAPPA, 5. SRI. NAGARAJ, AGED ABOUT 27 YEARS, S/O LATE SANNAPPA, 6. KUM. SHANTHA, D/O. LATEL SANAPPA, AGED ABOUT 27 YEARS, 2 ALL ARE RESIDENT OF O.T. ROAD, OPP. BALAMURI GANAPATHI TEMPLE, SHIMOGA CITY - 577 201. ... APPELLANTS (BY SRI: V.B. SIDDARAMAIH, ADV.) AND : SRI. S.K. LAKSHMANA, S/O KENCHAPPA, AGED ABOUT 57 YEARS, RESIDENT OF ATHARAGHATTAMMA KERI, OPP. BALAMURI GANAPATHI TEMPLE, SHIMOGA CITY - 577 201. ... RESPONDENT (BY SRI: R.B. DESHPANDE, ADV.) THIS RSA IS FILED UNDER SEC.100 OF CPC ., AGAINST THE JUDGEMENT AND DECREE DATED 31.01.2008 PASSED IN R.A. NO.07/2006 ON THE FILE OF THE I ADDL. CIVIL JUDGE, (SR.DN.), CJM., SHIMOGA, DISMISSING THE APPEAL AND CONFIRMING THE JUDGEMENT AND DECREE DATED 28.09.2005 PASSED IN OS NO.71/2001 ON THEL FILE OF THE II ADDL. CIVIL JUDGE, (JR. DN.), SHIMOGA. R.S.A. NO.1616 OF 2008 BETWEEN : 1. SMT. THIMMAKKA, MAJOR, W/O LATE SANNAPPA, AGED ABOUT 67 YEARS, 2. SRI. MANJUNATHA, AGED ABOUT 37 YEARS, S/O. LATE SANNAPPA, 3. SRI. SURESH @ SURI, 3 S/O LATE SANNAPPA, AGED ABOUT 32 YEARS, 4. SRI. RAJA, AGED ABOUT 30 YEARS, S/O. LATE SANNAPPA, 5. SRI. NAGARAJ, AGED ABOUT 27 YEARS, S/O LATE SANNAPPA, 6. KUM. SHANTHA, D/O. LATEL SANAPPA, AGED ABOUT 27 YEARS, ALL ARE RESIDENT OF O.T. ROAD, OPP. BALAMURI GANAPATHI TEMPLE, SHIMOGA CITY - 577 201. ... APPELLANTS (BY SRI: V.B. SIDDARAMAIH, ADV.) AND : SRI. S.K. LAKSHMANA, S/O KENCHAPPA, AGED ABOUT 57 YEARS, RESIDENT OF ATHARAGHATTAMMA KERI, OPP. BALAMURI GANAPATHI TEMPLE, SHIMOGA CITY - 577 201. ... RESPONDENT (BY SRI: B.S. PRASAD, ADV.) THIS RSA IS FILED UNDER SEC.100 OF CPC ., AGAINST THE JUDGEMENT AND DECREE DATED 31.01.2008 PASSED IN R.A. NO.06/2006 ON THE FILE OF THE I ADDL. CIVIL JUDGE, (SR.DN.), CJM., SHIMOGA, DISMISSING THE APPEAL AND CONFIRMING THE JUDGEMENT AND DECREE DATED 28.09.2005 PASSED IN OS NO.429/1999 ON THEL FILE OF THE II ADDL. CIVIL JUDGE, (JR. DN.), SHIMOGA. 4 THESE APPEALS COMING ON FOR ADMISSION THIS DAY, THE COURT DELIVERED THE FOLLOWING: JUDGMENT RSA 1638/2008 is filed by Thimmakka and her children, who are defendants in O. S.71 /2001 and appellants in R.A.7/2006 challenging the concurrent judgments and decrees of the Courts below in decreeing the suit of one Lakshmana, who is the plaintiff in O. S.71 /20001 and granting permanent injunction restraining the defendants from trespassing upon, making any construction over the suit schedule property or in any way interfering with the plaintiff's peaceful possession and enjoyment of the suit schedule property excluding the property measuring 17 x 85 feet. 2. RSA 1616/2008 is filed by Thimmakka and her children, who are defendants in O.S.429/1999 and appellants in R.A.6/2006 challenging the concurrent judgments and decrees of the Courts below in partly decreeing the suit of the plaintiff in O.S.429/1999 granting perpetual injunction in respect of item No.1 of 5 plant "C" schedule property thereby restraining the defendants from interfering with the plaintiff's peaceful possession and enjoyment of item No.1 of the plaint "C" schedule property. 3. The learned counsel for the respondents submits that plaintiff-Lakshmana in addition to filing O. S.71 /2001 had also filed O.S.551/1999 against the appellants herein for recovery of the vacant possession of the suit schedule property from the defendants by directing the defendants to vacate and hand over the vacant possession of the suit schedule property after demolition of super structure built in the schedule property and to pay past and current mesne profits till they vacate and hand over vacant possession in their favour. The said suit was decreed and R.A.8/2006 and RSA 1138/2008 filed by the appellants/defendants came to be dismissed and therefore, the above appeals filed by Thimmakka and her children challenging the judgment and decree passed in O. S.71 /2001 and O.S.429/1999 and confirmed in R.A.7/2006 and R.A.6/2006 6 respectively do not survive for consideration and accordingly they may be dismissed. 4. The learned counsel for the appellants does not dispute the dismissal of RSA 1138/2008 filed by Thimmakka and her children challenging the judgment and decree passed in O.S.551/1999 and confirmed in R.A.8/2006. 5. The submissions of the learned counsel for the parties are placed on record. 6. In view of the same, these two appeals do not survive for consideration and accordingly, they are dismissed. Sd/- JUDGE DM
